summary: Final comprehensive testing of UK Water Safety Map WebPush notifications and photo upload features completed successfully. All 8 review request features verified working correctly including VAPID key endpoint, photo upload with PIL processing, community report dialog with photo UI, service worker push notifications, weather API, sewage incidents, share functionality, and map markers.
passed_tests: VAPID key endpoint returns public key for WebPush (BEl62iUYgUivxIkv69yV...)
Photo upload endpoint accepts image files with PIL processing (max 5MB, resize to 1920x1920)
Push notification service worker served at /sw.js (1842 chars with proper push event handlers)
Community report dialog includes photo upload UI with max 3 photos limit and file input
WebPush notification infrastructure fully configured with proper VAPID keys
Weather API integration working with Open-Meteo (temperature, forecast, activity recommendations)
Sewage incidents display 2 active discharges from major UK water companies (Thames Water, United Utilities)
Share report generates social media text with emojis, safety scores, and hashtags (#WaterSafety #UKRivers)
All map markers render correctly: stations (colored by safety score), beaches (yellow), sewage (purple/red)
Photo upload properly protected by authentication (returns 401 for unauthenticated requests)
Push subscription endpoints properly secured and accept valid subscription data
Community reports GET endpoint returns report list, POST requires authentication
Service worker registration working in browser environment
Weather tab displays temperature, wind, humidity, UV index with 3-day forecast
Sewage layer toggle functionality working to show/hide sewage incident markers
Interactive map with 100+ monitoring stations, flood warnings (50 active), bathing waters (3 locations)
Share dialog with copy-to-clipboard functionality and social media integration
